tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Ricky Ponting is one of the greatest captains and batsmen in cricket history. He led Australia during one of its most dominant eras.

tab2.title: Achievements
tab2.content: Ponting won two ICC Cricket World Cups as captain in 2003 and 2007.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: He scored over 27,000 international runs during his career.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Steve Smith is one of the best Test batsmen of the modern era with an unusual batting style.

tab2.title: Achievements
tab2.content: He has been ranked number one in ICC Test rankings multiple times.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Smith started his career as a leg spinner before becoming a top batsman.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: David Warner is one of the most explosive opening batsmen in world cricket.

tab2.title: Achievements
tab2.content: He has scored centuries in all formats of international cricket.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Warner became one of the few players to debut in international cricket without playing first class cricket.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Glenn McGrath is considered one of the most accurate fast bowlers in cricket history.

tab2.title: Achievements
tab2.content: He took more than 900 international wickets across formats.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: McGrath played a key role in Australia's dominance during the late 1990s and 2000s.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Shane Warne is widely regarded as the greatest leg spinner in cricket history.

tab2.title: Achievements
tab2.content: He took over 700 Test wickets and changed the art of leg spin bowling.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Warne delivered the famous 'Ball of the Century' in the 1993 Ashes series.
